factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with organic sales increases in some segments but declines in others.

evidence.text: Segment net sales of $343.7 million in the fourth quarter of 2018 increased $2.0 million, or 0.6%, from 2017 levels, reflecting an $11.5 million, or 3.5%, organic sales gain and $0.4 million of acquisition-related sales, partially offset by $9.9 million of unfavorable foreign currency translation.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are mixed with some segments showing growth and others declines.

factors.summary: Pricing power is under some pressure as average yields on finance receivables have slightly decreased.

evidence.text: In the fourth quarters of 2018 and 2017, the respective average yields on finance receivables were 17.7% and 17.8%, and the average yields on contract receivables were 9.2% in both periods.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Average yields on finance receivables have slightly decreased, indicating moderate pressure on pricing power.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin products, negatively impacting gross margins.

evidence.text: Gross margin of 38.5% in the quarter declined 80 bps from 39.3% last year primarily due to higher sales volumes of lower gross margin products and higher material and other costs, partially offset by benefits from the company’s RCI initiatives.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin products, negatively impacting gross margins.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed with some segments showing growth while others decline.

evidence.text: Segment net sales of $407.4 million in the fourth quarter of 2018 decreased $1.8 million, or 0.4%, from 2017 levels, reflecting a $1.6 million, or 0.4%, organic sales increase, which was more than offset by $3.4 million of unfavorable foreign currency translation.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is mixed with some segments showing growth while others decline.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed with some areas showing growth and others experiencing declines.

evidence.text: Segment net sales of $339.9 million in the fourth quarter of 2018 decreased $16.9 million, or 4.7%, from 2017 levels, reflecting a $12.2 million, or 3.5%, organic sales decline and $4.7 million of unfavorable foreign currency translation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends show a decrease in some areas, reflecting a 4.7% decline in segment net sales.

factors.summary: End market momentum varies with growth in financial services and some industrial segments but decline in others.

evidence.text: Financial services revenue of $82.7 million in the fourth quarter of 2018 increased $2.8 million, or 3.5%, from $79.9 million last year primarily reflecting $3.1 million of higher revenue as a result of continued growth of the company’s financial services portfolio.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End market momentum varies with growth in some segments but decline in others.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, putting pressure on margins.

evidence.text: Gross margin of 38.5% in the quarter declined 80 bps from 39.3% last year primarily due to higher sales volumes of lower gross margin products and higher material and other costs, partially offset by benefits from the company’s RCI initiatives.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising, putting pressure on margins due to higher material and other costs.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to higher costs and unfavorable foreign currency effects.

evidence.text: Gross margin of 38.5% in the quarter declined 80 bps from 39.3% last year primarily due to higher sales volumes of lower gross margin products and higher material and other costs, partially offset by benefits from the company’s RCI initiatives.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to higher costs and unfavorable foreign currency effects.

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are helping to offset some cost pressures.

evidence.text: Gross margin of 38.5% in the quarter declined 80 bps from 39.3% last year primarily due to higher sales volumes of lower gross margin products and higher material and other costs, partially offset by benefits from the company’s RCI initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity initiatives are offsetting cost pressures, indicating moderate improvement.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable foreign currency translation and economic conditions impacting sales and costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable foreign currency and economic conditions pose significant risks, impacting sales and costs.
